draft:
I am writing to provide my highest recommendation for Ms. Neha Kulkarni, a highly accomplished biopharmaceutical executive and innovator in the field of infectious diseases. As the former CEO of Biotronix, I had the privilege of working closely with Ms. Kulkarni and witnessing firsthand her extraordinary contributions to the development of novel therapies for multidrug-resistant infections. 

To provide some background, I am currently the Head of Therapeutics at GeneAnalytics, where I lead our drug discovery programs across multiple disease areas, leveraging insights from our genetic research platform. I joined GeneAnalytics in 2019 after serving as the Chief Executive Officer of Biotronix from 2011 to 2018. Biotronix was a biopharmaceutical company focused on the development of innovative antibacterial agents to address the growing threat of multidrug-resistant gram-negative infections. As CEO, I was responsible for overseeing all aspects of the company's operations, including research and development, clinical trials, regulatory affairs, and business development. 

Prior to my role at Biotronix, I spent over 15 years at BioGenics, where I held various leadership positions in research and development. From 2010 to 2011, I served as Senior Vice President and Head of Product Development for Asia Pacific, overseeing Apex Pharmaceuticals' drug development strategy in the region. Before that, I was Senior Vice President of Clinical Development for Inflammation, leading the development of therapies for autoimmune and inflammatory diseases. I also held roles as Vice President of Development Sciences, Vice President of Research Operations and Pathology, and Vice President of Immunology, Tissue Growth and Repair. During my tenure at BioGenics, I contributed to the development of several successful therapies, including monoclonal antibodies and small molecules for the treatment of cancer and autoimmune diseases. 

I hold an MB ChB degree in Medicine from the University of Edinburgh, Scotland, and am a Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians (FRCP) and the Royal College of Pathologists (FRCP). I have authored numerous scientific publications and am a named inventor on nearly 50 issued patents. My extensive experience in drug development, coupled with my clinical background as a surgeon and pathologist, has provided me with a deep understanding of the challenges and opportunities in bringing innovative therapies to patients. 

It was during my tenure as CEO of Biotronix that I had the opportunity to work closely with Ms. Kulkarni and witness her exceptional talents and contributions firsthand. Ms. Kulkarni joined Biotronix in 2012 as a Research Associate and quickly demonstrated her extraordinary capabilities, earning four promotions over six years to become Associate Director, Assay Sciences and Innovation, and Head of Research Operations. In this role, she led two critical departments responsible for research and early development operations, as well as the development of new technologies and assays to identify next-generation medicines for infectious diseases. 

One of Ms. Kulkarni's most significant achievements at Biotronix was her leadership in the development of a cutting-edge antibody discovery technology. This innovative platform, which utilized gel encapsulation and microfluidics, enabled the rapid identification of antibodies that bind to cell surface epitopes of pathogenic microorganisms. The technology was particularly effective in discovering antibodies against multidrug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria, such as Enterobacter cloacae and Klebsiella pneumoniae, which pose significant public health threats due to their resistance to existing antibiotics. 

Ms. Kulkarni's pioneering work in this area was recognized by the Solari Foundation, which awarded Biotronix $10.5 million in grant funding and made a $10 million equity investment to support the development of monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) against serious, multidrug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria. The initial grant funded Biotronix's program to discover mAbs against Enterobacter cloacae, a major cause of neonatal sepsis. If successful, the program had the potential to receive additional grants from the Solari Foundation for further antibody discovery and development initiatives. 

In addition to her technical contributions, Ms. Kulkarni demonstrated outstanding leadership and project management skills. She spearheaded the formation of a cross-functional team of 20 scientists to develop the antibody discovery technology and drive the program forward. Her ability to foster collaboration and effectively communicate the project's goals and progress to stakeholders was instrumental in securing the Solari Foundation's support. 

Beyond the antibody discovery program, Ms. Kulkarni also led a $2 million bacterial genetics project aimed at creating a novel, cost-effective starting point for one of Biotronix's lead candidate antibiotics. She collaborated with chemists, microbiologists, and key opinion leaders to establish the company's bacterial genetic engineering expertise, further solidifying Biotronix's position as a leader in the fight against antibiotic-resistant infections. 

Throughout her tenure at Biotronix, Ms. Kulkarni consistently demonstrated her ability to integrate scientific and business perspectives to drive innovation and create organizational value. She established and managed two successful business units with a cumulative budget of approximately $5 million: a scientific team responsible for assay development, screening, and lead identification, and an operations team responsible for scalable research. As a member of the Scientific Review Committee, she advised on drug discovery and screening strategies and provided valuable input on portfolio governance. 

Ms. Kulkarni's contributions extended beyond her direct responsibilities, as she played a key role in shaping Biotronix's corporate culture and fostering an inclusive and high-performance work environment. She conceptualized and implemented a pilot research internship program that provided hands-on biotechnology and management experience to a cohort of student interns and mentors. As the leader of the corporate culture team, she conducted several initiatives to boost morale and inclusivity, resulting in a 20% improvement in survey ratings for key areas such as decision-making and camaraderie. Her efforts to engage and motivate the workforce were instrumental in maintaining a strong and cohesive team during a period of rapid growth and innovation. 

In summary, Ms. Kulkarni's tenure at Biotronix was marked by remarkable achievements and contributions that had a lasting impact on the company's success and its mission to combat multidrug-resistant infections. Her leadership in developing the antibody discovery platform and securing significant funding from the Solari Foundation demonstrated her ability to drive innovation and deliver results. Her technical expertise, strategic vision, and commitment to fostering a positive corporate culture made her an invaluable asset to Biotronix and a respected leader in the field of infectious disease research. It was truly a privilege to work alongside Ms. Kulkarni and witness her extraordinary talents firsthand. 

Since leaving Biotronix, Ms. Kulkarni has continued to excel in her career, further solidifying her reputation as a leader in the biotechnology industry. She joined Evora Therapeutics in 2019 as Associate Director of Portfolio Planning and Program Management and was quickly promoted to Director in 2023. In this role, she has led strategic planning and governance reviews for a Serano-partnered Phase 1/2 program aimed at treating frontotemporal dementia (FTD). Her efforts have successfully advanced an Alzheimer's disease program in partnership with Serano from early-stage discovery to CTA filing and Phase 1, resulting in Serano's decision to opt-in to co-develop and co-commercialize the clinical candidate, as well as several million dollars in milestone payments to Evora. 

Ms. Kulkarni's expertise in business development has been instrumental in Evora's growth and success. She collaborated closely with the Chief Scientific Officer and Chief Business Officer to develop the company's business strategy and research plans, leading to the successful introduction of pioneering gene therapy programs. She also established and maintained productive strategic alliances with international industry and academic partners to drive the discovery of novel CNS-directed AAV-based gene therapies. 

In addition to her professional accomplishments, Ms. Kulkarni has demonstrated her commitment to advancing the field of biotechnology through her involvement in various leadership roles. She has served as a Biotechnology Advisory Board Member at Illinois State University since 2016, providing valuable insights and guidance to the academic community. From 2016 to 2019, she was an Industry Advisor for the IGNITE Translational Research Program at Harvard Medical School, where she supported the development of innovative therapies and technologies. 

Ms. Kulkarni's passion for mentoring the next generation of scientists and leaders is evident in her extensive involvement in STEM outreach programs. She has led corporate initiatives that have motivated over 1,000 young students from underrepresented and underserved communities, inspiring them to pursue careers in the life sciences. Her dedication to fostering diversity and inclusion in the industry is truly commendable. 

In conclusion, Ms. Neha Kulkarni is an exceptional biopharmaceutical executive whose contributions to the field of infectious diseases and neurodegenerative disorders have been truly transformative. Her leadership, strategic vision, and technical expertise have driven significant advancements in the development of novel therapies, particularly in the fight against multidrug-resistant infections. Her ability to integrate scientific and business perspectives has consistently created organizational value and optimized portfolios, positioning her as a respected leader in the biotechnology industry. 

Throughout her career, Ms. Kulkarni has demonstrated an unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence. Her pioneering work in antibody discovery and her successful collaborations with global partners, such as the Solari Foundation, have had a profound impact on public health. Her ongoing efforts at Evora Therapeutics, where she has spearheaded critical programs in neurodegenerative diseases, further underscore her remarkable capabilities and dedication to improving patient outcomes. 

Beyond her professional achievements, Ms. Kulkarni's passion for mentoring and fostering diversity in the life sciences is truly commendable. Her extensive involvement in STEM outreach and her leadership roles in academic and industry advisory boards reflect her commitment to nurturing the next generation of scientists and leaders. 

It has been a privilege to witness Ms. Kulkarni's extraordinary talents and contributions firsthand. Her remarkable achievements and unwavering dedication to her field make her an invaluable asset to any organization. I have no doubt that she will continue to make significant contributions to the biotechnology industry and to the advancement of global health.
final:
Dear Sir or Madam, 

 I am writing to provide my highest recommendation for Ms. Neha Kulkarni, a highly accomplished biopharmaceutical executive and innovator in the field of neurodegenerative disorders and infectious diseases. 

 To provide some background, I recently served as the Head of Therapeutics at GeneAnalytics, where I led our drug discovery programs across multiple disease areas, leveraging insights from our genetic research platform. I joined GeneAnalytics in 2019 after serving in various roles at Biotronix from 2011 to 2018, including as Chief Executive Officer and President of R&D. Biotronix is a biopharmaceutical company focused on the development of innovative antibacterial agents to address the growing threat of multidrug-resistant gram-negative infections. Before joining Biotronix, I spent over 15 years at BioGenics in various leadership roles in R&D. From 2010 to 2011, I was the Senior VP and Head of Product Development for Asia Pacific, managing Apex Pharmaceuticals' drug development strategy. I previously served as Senior VP of Clinical Development for Inflammation and held several VP positions in Development Sciences, Research Operations and Pathology, and Immunology. I contributed to developing successful therapies for cancer and autoimmune diseases. 

 It was during my tenure at Biotronix that I had the opportunity to work closely with Ms. Kulkarni and witness her remarkable talents and contributions firsthand. Ms. Kulkarni joined Biotronix in 2012 as a Research Associate and quickly demonstrated her extraordinary capabilities, earning four promotions over six years to become Associate Director, Assay Sciences and Innovation, and Head of Research Operations. In this role, she led two critical departments responsible for research and early development operations, as well as the development of new technologies and assays to identify next-generation medicines for infectious diseases. 

 One of Ms. Kulkarni's most significant achievements at Biotronix was her leadership in the development of a cutting-edge antibody discovery technology. This innovative platform, which utilized gel encapsulation and microfluidics, enabled the rapid identification of antibodies that bind to cell surface epitopes of pathogenic microorganisms. The technology was particularly effective in discovering antibodies against multidrug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria, such as Enterobacter cloacae and Klebsiella pneumoniae, which pose significant public health threats due to their resistance to existing antibiotics. 

Ms. Kulkarni's pioneering work in this area was recognized by the Solari Foundation, which awarded Biotronix $10.5 million in grant funding and made a $10 million equity investment to support the development of monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) against serious, multidrug-resistant Gram-negative bacteria. The initial grant funded Biotronix's program to discover mAbs against Enterobacter cloacae, a major cause of neonatal sepsis. If successful, the program had the potential to receive additional grants from the Solari Foundation for further antibody discovery and development initiatives. 

Ms. Kulkarni made significant technical contributions while showcasing remarkable leadership and project management abilities. She led the creation of a 20-member cross-functional team to advance antibody discovery technology, excelling in collaboration and stakeholder communication, which was crucial in securing support from the Solari Foundation.

In addition to her work on antibody discovery, Ms. Kulkarni spearheaded a $2 million bacterial genetics project. This initiative aimed to develop a novel, cost-effective starting point for one of Biotronix's leading antibiotic candidates. By working closely with chemists, microbiologists, and industry experts, she helped establish the company's expertise in bacterial genetic engineering, reinforcing Biotronix's leadership in combating antibiotic-resistant infections. 

Throughout her time at Biotronix, Ms. Kulkarni consistently integrated scientific and business insights to drive innovation and organizational value. She successfully managed two business units with a combined budget of approximately $5 million. Moreover, as part of the Scientific Review Committee, she advised on drug discovery and screening strategies, providing valuable input on portfolio governance. 

Ms. Kulkarni's efforts towards the development of these technologies have resulted in granted patents, underscoring her innovative contributions to the field. One such achievement is her development of the patented revolutionary antibody screening method aimed at combating global antibiotic-resistant bacterial infections. This technology provides a robust framework for identifying effective antibodies that target resistant strains, significantly enhancing the tools available to healthcare professionals in addressing one of the world's most urgent medical challenges. 

Her pioneering work also includes the development of an immunoassay that guides the dosing of Orgamycin, known commercially as GENRX. This WHO essential medicine has become a critical therapeutic option for treating serious bacterial infections, particularly complicated urinary tract infections. Ms. Kulkarni's expertise in optimizing the dosing regimen through precise immunoassay development was crucial in ensuring the drug’s optimal efficacy and safety, contributing to its FDA approval and inclusion in the World Health Organization's List of Essential Medicines. 

These patents not only highlight Ms. Kulkarni's technical proficiency and innovative spirit but also demonstrate her commitment to advancing public health solutions on a global scale. Her contributions ensure that critical medicines are accessible to patients in need, with precise dosing and targeted efficacy against resistant infections. 

In summary, Ms. Neha Kulkarni exemplifies leadership, innovation, and dedication in advancing biopharmaceutical solutions for neurodegenerative disorders and infectious diseases. Her work at Biotronix, from antibody discovery to patented technologies, highlights her outstanding ability to drive projects from concept to fruition. Her technical expertise, coupled with her strategic acumen, has significantly impacted the development of critical treatments, such as the revolutionary antibody screening method and the crucial immunoassay for Orgamycin (GENRX). These contributions not only underscore her profound impact on the field but also reflect her unwavering commitment to improving global health outcomes. It has been a privilege to witness Ms. Kulkarni's extraordinary talents and contributions firsthand. Her remarkable achievements and unwavering dedication to her field make her an invaluable asset to any organization. I have no doubt that she will continue to make significant contributions to the biotechnology industry and to the advancement of global health.
